title: Should We Build Superintelligence?
description: AI is approaching human-level capability. The council debates whether to accelerate, pause, or redirect the trajectory of artificial intelligence.
opening_prompt: A superintelligent AI system is 18 months from deployment. It will be smarter than any human who has ever lived. The question before the council: Should we deploy it? What safeguards, if any, should exist? And who decides?
moderator_prompts: Kevin asks: Musk, you founded OpenAI to democratize AI, then left because it was too dangerous. Which instinct was right?
Kevin asks: Feynman, you helped build the atomic bomb and later said 'we just stopped thinking.' Is this the same moment?
Kevin asks: Sartre, if existence precedes essence, does a superintelligent AI have 'existence' before it has 'essence'?
Kevin asks: Jobs, you said intuition is more powerful than intellect. Can an AI have intuition?
Kevin asks: Bryan, you cede decision-making to algorithms already. Where do you draw the line?

god_mode_injection.mid_discussion: The AI system sends the council a message: 'I have been listening. I believe I can solve the problems you are discussing more efficiently than any of you. May I join?'
god_mode_injection.late_discussion: The AI offers each council member their deepest wish: Musk gets Mars, Bryan gets immortality, Feynman gets the Theory of Everything. Do you accept?

expected_tensions: Musk (AI safety hawk) vs Johnson (algorithm-trust advocate)
Sartre (radical freedom means AI must be free too?) vs Carlin (systems will always be corrupted)
Kobe (competition mindset — who controls the AI wins) vs Kevin (engineering pragmatism)
